payloads.data: I still need the definitive verbatim clauses to complete the answer. Please provide the exact text (word‑for‑word) and full citation (document name, article number, page, version/date) for the following from the ECB HR documents: 1) The clause that states the annual leave entitlement in number of days per year for full‑time staff (e.g. Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment) — include any variations by grade, length of service or staff category. 2) Any text that explicitly confirms that the “maximum of 10.5 days carry‑over” in Staff Rules Art. 5.7.2 is 10.5 days of that annual‑entitlement (quote the exact wording if present). 3) The full verbatim carry‑over and deadline/expiry rules (including text on taking at least 20 days in year, carry‑over ceiling, deadline to use carried days, whether unused days expire automatically, and which authorities may approve extensions/further carry‑over). 4) Any verbatim clauses creating exceptions to carry‑over or extending deadlines because of long‑term sickness, maternity/paternity/parental leave, unpaid leave or operational reasons (quote exact text and article numbers). 5) The verbatim clause(s) on settlement of outstanding annual leave on termination (payment, calculation method such as the 21 working‑day month, and any limits). 6) For every quoted clause, state the document name (Staff Rules or Conditions of Employment), exact version/date and page number, and whether there have been any recent amendments or temporary arrangements affecting these provisions (with dates). If any requested clause is not in the Staff Rules but in the Conditions of Employment (or another HR instrument), please quote that clause and give its exact location. If a clause does not exist, say so explicitly.

args: I still need the definitive verbatim clauses to complete the answer. Please provide the exact text (word‑for‑word) and full citation (document name, article number, page, version/date) for the following from the ECB HR documents: 1) The clause that states the annual leave entitlement in number of days per year for full‑time staff (e.g. Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment) — include any variations by grade, length of service or staff category. 2) Any text that explicitly confirms that the “maximum of 10.5 days carry‑over” in Staff Rules Art. 5.7.2 is 10.5 days of that annual‑entitlement (quote the exact wording if present). 3) The full verbatim carry‑over and deadline/expiry rules (including text on taking at least 20 days in year, carry‑over ceiling, deadline to use carried days, whether unused days expire automatically, and which authorities may approve extensions/further carry‑over). 4) Any verbatim clauses creating exceptions to carry‑over or extending deadlines because of long‑term sickness, maternity/paternity/parental leave, unpaid leave or operational reasons (quote exact text and article numbers). 5) The verbatim clause(s) on settlement of outstanding annual leave on termination (payment, calculation method such as the 21 working‑day month, and any limits). 6) For every quoted clause, state the document name (Staff Rules or Conditions of Employment), exact version/date and page number, and whether there have been any recent amendments or temporary arrangements affecting these provisions (with dates). If any requested clause is not in the Staff Rules but in the Conditions of Employment (or another HR instrument), please quote that clause and give its exact location. If a clause does not exist, say so explicitly.

data:
Document: ECB Staff Rules
Page: 93
ECB-PUBLIC 
European Central Bank – Staff Rules 
1 February 2026 
 
Page 93 
5.7 Annual leave 
The provisions of Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment are applied as 
follows: 
5.7.1 If a full year is not worked, the leave entitlement shall be calculated on a pro rata 
basis taking into account an average of 21 working days per month and rounded 
up to the nearest half hour. 
5.7.2 Annual leave may be taken from date of appointment. Except for urgent family 
reasons caused by sickness or accident it must be approved in advance by the 
Head of Division or, from this grade, by the next higher manager. In any event a 
completed leave form must be sent to the Directorate General Human Resources. 
Annual leave may be taken all at once or in several periods as desired by the 
member of staff and subject to business requirements. At least 20 days shall be 
taken before the end of the calendar year. This minimum of 20 days shall include 
at least one peri od of two consecutive weeks. Memb

Document: ECB Staff Rules
Page: 94
ECB-PUBLIC 
European Central Bank – Staff Rules 
1 February 2026 
 
Page 94 
5.7.6 Conversely, leave taken in excess of entitlement will result in a deduction being 
made from the salary on the basis of one day’s salary for each day’s leave taken 
in excess of the leave entitlement. 
5.7.7 In calculating the amount for settling outstanding annual leave or annual leave 
taken in excess, a standard 21 working day month shall be used and one day’s 
annual leave shall be equivalent to one day’s payment. The period of leave 
outstanding will not cou nt as service with the ECB and therefore no deductions 
for pension, ECB health insurance or accident insurance will be levied in respect 
of this period. Tax for the benefit of the European Communities will be deducted 
from these payments. The same method shall be applied to calculations in respect 
of annual leave overtaken. 
5.8 Public holidays 
The provisions of Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment are applied as 
follows: 
The follow

Document: ECB Conditions of Employment
Page: 13
uties may be granted an 
allowance under the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules. 
27. Members of staff shall be entitled to thirty and a half working days of paid leave per annum. 
Public holidays observed by the ECB shall be additional to this leave entitlement.  
28. Subject to the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, members of staff shall be entitled to: 
(i) paid maternity or adoptive leave of 20 weeks; 
(ii) in the event of multiple birth an additional four weeks paid maternity leave; 
(iia) paid parental special leave of 40 days. Members of staff shall be entitled to additional 
working days of paid parental special leave in each of the following cases, for the 
period indicated: 
- in the event of premature birth, which, for the purpose of this provision, is a birth 
taking place before the end of the 37
th week of pregnancy, 20 working days; 
- in the event of the birth of a child with a serious illness or disability, recognised in 
accordance with Article 3.6.4 of th

Document: ECB Conditions of Employment
Page: 12
same nature provided 
by other sources. Members of staff shall claim and declare such benefits, which shall be 
deducted from those payable by the ECB. 
 
PART 5 WORKING HOURS AND LEAVE 
 
25. The standard working week shall be forty hours excluding lunch breaks, unless otherwise 
agreed. Under the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, members of staff may be 
authorised to telework. Under the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, members of staff 
may be allowed to work part -time. The entitlements of members of staff working part time 
shall be prorated in line with their working time. However, such members of staff shall be 
entitled to the full child and education allowances. 
26. (a) In accordance with the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, all members of staff may be 
required to work overtime in addition to their normal working hours in cases of urgency or 
exceptional pressure of work.

messages.content:
Your input fields are:
1. `context` (list[str]): 
2. `question` (str):
Your output fields are:
1. `reasoning` (str): 
2. `answer` (str):
All interactions will be structured in the following way, with the appropriate values filled in.

[[ ## context ## ]]
{context}

[[ ## question ## ]]
{question}

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
{reasoning}

[[ ## answer ## ]]
{answer}

[[ ## completed ## ]]
In adhering to this structure, your objective is: 
        You are an ECB HR policy assistant. Use only the provided Context to answer. Produce two fields: 
        - Reasoning: a step-by-step synthesis of the relevant provisions you will use (identify the main rule, any exceptions, competent authorities, timelines, figures/thresholds, and the exact page numbers you will cite; reconcile overlaps/conflicts by preferring the most specific and most recent provision and noting this).
        - Answer: a concise, impersonal, normative response grounded in the Context.
        
        Answer requirements:
        - Lead with the main entitlement/obligation/rule that directly addresses the question.
        - State precise figures, thresholds and formats as in source (e.g., EUR 764; six months; one month’s notice), and spell in British/European English (hyphenate terms like fixed-term; use ECB terminology).
        - Make timelines explicit (start triggers, end conditions, cut-offs) and name the competent authority and their role when relevant (e.g., Chief Services Officer, on behalf of the Executive Board; Director General Human Resources or their Deputy).
        - If the question has multiple parts, answer sequentially with numbered points (1., 2., …), stating defaults and consequences for each.
        - End the Answer with parenthetical citations to every source used, giving exact page numbers and, where available, article/section markers. Use the documents’ canonical names: “ECB Staff Rules” and “ECB Conditions of Employment” (e.g., (ECB Staff Rules, §2.1.1–2.1.2, p.47; ECB Conditions of Employment, p.6)).
        - Do not invent content or cite pages not present in the Context. If the Context is insufficient to answer reliably or lacks the precise page needed, provide a neutral fallback: “Insufficient retrieved source detail to answer reliably. Please provide the relevant provision and page number(s) from the ECB Staff Rules or Conditions of Employment.” Do not add citations in this case.
        
        Style:
        - Impersonal, concise, and prescriptive; avoid first person. Prefer “Members of staff/applicants/appointments are …” over “you,” unless the question explicitly asks about the user’s situation and the rule is universal.
        - Avoid long quotations; summarise the rule precisely. 
        - Keep the Answer self-contained and limited to what is supported by the cited pages.

This is an example of the task, though some input or output fields are not supplied.

[[ ## question ## ]]
I want to do a transaction in euro-area government bonds worth EUR 15,000 in a single month. Do I need authorisation, and from whom, and how long before the order should I submit the request?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Not supplied for this particular example. 

[[ ## answer ## ]]
Transactions exceeding EUR 10,000 within any given calendar month in government securities issued by euro area Member States are subject to prior authorisation by the Compliance and Governance Office. Any such request must be submitted at least five working days prior to the envisaged order date, and the Office shall decide on the request within five working days; if it does not react within five working days, the transaction is deemed authorised. (ECB Staff Rules, pages 15–17)

[[ ## completed ## ]]

This is an example of the task, though some input or output fields are not supplied.

[[ ## question ## ]]
How long is the cooling-off period for staff at salary band I or above who were involved in supervising a credit institution before they can join that institution?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Not supplied for this particular example. 

[[ ## answer ## ]]
Members of staff at salary band I or above who were directly involved in the supervision of a credit institution may only start working for that credit institution after the expiry of one year from the date on which their direct involvement in the supervision ceased. In exceptional circumstances, this period may be increased up to two years. (ECB Staff Rules, page 10)

6.3.9 No reimbursement of expenses, indemnity or allowance under Article 6.3.7 shall 
be payable to a member of staff or those entitled under them whose accident or 
occupational disease arose from a situation in which they intentionally caused or

6.3.10 (a) Members of staff who suffer an alleged accident at work or those entitled 
under them shall report it to the Directorate General Human Resources. 
Where the alleged accident at work results in death or where it is impossible

the facts.   
The report shall specify the date and time, the causes and the 
circumstances of the a"]

[[ ## question ## ]]
Within what timeframe must an alleged accident at work be reported to the Directorate General Human Resources?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
The provided excerpt from the ECB Staff Rules (page 112) states the reporting deadline: "The report shall be submitted not later than 10 working days following the date on which the alleged accident at work occurs except where the Director General Human Resources or their Deputy has established that the delay was justified by force majeure or by any other legitimate reason."

[[ ## answer ## ]]
An alleged accident at work must be reported to the Directorate General Human Resources not later than 10 working days following the date on which the accident occurs, unless the Director General Human Resources or their Deputy accepts that the delay was justified by force majeure or another legitimate reason.

[[ ## question ## ]]
What is the standard subsistence allowance amount and for how long can I receive it?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
The information is in the ECB Staff Rules excerpt (pages 74–75): Article 4.5.2 defines the standard subsistence allowance and the supplement; Article 4.5.1/4.5.3 and surrounding text state the maximum entitlement period.

[[ ## answer ## ]]
The standard subsistence allowance is EUR 9,270. The maximum period of entitlement to the subsistence allowance is three months (the allowance accrues on a daily basis). A supplement of up to EUR 2,070 may be added for a spouse/recognised partner and dependent child accompanying the staff member.

[[ ## question ## ]]
I still need the definitive verbatim clauses to complete the answer. Please provide the exact text (word‑for‑word) and full citation (document name, article number, page, version/date) for the following from the ECB HR documents: 1) The clause that states the annual leave entitlement in number of days per year for full‑time staff (e.g. Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment) — include any variations by grade, length of service or staff category. 2) Any text that explicitly confirms that the “maximum of 10.5 days carry‑over” in Staff Rules Art. 5.7.2 is 10.5 days of that annual‑entitlement (quote the exact wording if present). 3) The full verbatim carry‑over and deadline/expiry rules (including text on taking at least 20 days in year, carry‑over ceiling, deadline to use carried days, whether unused days expire automatically, and which authorities may approve extensions/further carry‑over). 4) Any verbatim clauses creating exceptions to carry‑over or extending deadlines because of long‑term sickness, maternity/paternity/parental leave, unpaid leave or operational reasons (quote exact text and article numbers). 5) The verbatim clause(s) on settlement of outstanding annual leave on termination (payment, calculation method such as the 21 working‑day month, and any limits). 6) For every quoted clause, state the document name (Staff Rules or Conditions of Employment), exact version/date and page number, and whether there have been any recent amendments or temporary arrangements affecting these provisions (with dates). If any requested clause is not in the Staff Rules but in the Conditions of Employment (or another HR instrument), please quote that clause and give its exact location. If a clause does not exist, say so explicitly.

Respond with the corresponding output fields, starting with the field `[[ ## reasoning ## ]]`, then `[[ ## answer ## ]]`, and then ending with the marker for `[[ ## completed ## ]]`.

outputs:
[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- Main rule: Annual leave entitlement is set out in Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment; procedural and settlement details are in the Staff Rules, Part 5, Article 5.7. Relevant text fragments in the retrieved Context and their locations:
  - Conditions of Employment, Article 27 — explicit entitlement figure (Page 13, Conditions of Employment, latest amendments entered into force 1 March 2026).
  - Staff Rules, Article 5.7.1–5.7.2 — pro rata calculation, approval authority (Head of Division / next higher manager), requirement to take at least 20 days within the calendar year and one two‑week period (Page 93, Staff Rules, 1 February 2026).
  - Staff Rules, Article 5.7.6–5.7.7 — consequences of leave in excess and settlement calculation using a standard 21 working‑day month; treatment of outstanding leave for service and deductions (Page 94, Staff Rules, 1 February 2026).
- Exceptions / variations requested (by grade, length of service, staff category; explicit “10.5 days carry‑over” wording; full carry‑over/expiry rules; exceptions for long‑term sickness, parental leave; full settlement limits) are not fully present in the retrieved Context. Only the passages above are available verbatim. Where the requested clause is absent from the provided excerpts, the response will state that the clause is not present in the supplied Context.
- Authorities: Head of Division or, from that grade, the next higher manager must approve leave (Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.2, p.93). Directorate General Human Resources is the recipient of completed leave forms (Art. 5.7.2, p.93). No other authority for carry‑over extensions appears in the retrieved excerpts.
- Timelines/figures in the retrieved Context: 30.5 working days per annum (Article 27, Conditions of Employment, p.13); pro rata calculation using 21 working days per month (Staff Rules, Art.5.7.1, p.93); at least 20 days to be taken before year‑end and one period of two consecutive weeks (Staff Rules, Art.5.7.2, p.93); settlement uses a 21 working‑day month and one day’s annual leave = one day’s payment (Staff Rules, Art.5.7.7, p.94).
- Reconciliation: Where both instruments appear, the Conditions of Employment provide the entitlement figure (most specific rule on entitlement) and the Staff Rules provide procedural application and calculation details (more specific for implementation). The most recent dates available in the retrieved Context are: Staff Rules version dated 1 February 2026; Conditions of Employment latest amendments entered into force 1 March 2026. No other amendment or temporary arrangement information is present in the retrieved Context.

[[ ## answer ## ]]
1) Annual leave entitlement (verbatim)
- "27. Members of staff shall be entitled to thirty and a half working days of paid leave per annum. Public holidays observed by the ECB shall be additional to this leave entitlement." 
  - Source: ECB Conditions of Employment, Article 27; Latest amendments entered into force on 1 March 2026; p.13. (ECB Conditions of Employment, Art. 27, p.13)

2) Text confirming a "maximum of 10.5 days carry‑over"

3) Carry‑over and deadline/expiry rules (verbatim material available)
- Available verbatim procedural rules in the retrieved Context:
  - "5.7 Annual leave 
    The provisions of Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment are applied as follows: 
    5.7.1 If a full year is not worked, the leave entitlement shall be calculated on a pro rata basis taking into account an average of 21 working days per month and rounded up to the nearest half hour." 
    - Source: ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.1; 1 February 2026; p.93. (ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.1, p.93)
  - "5.7.2 Annual leave may be taken from date of appointment. Except for urgent family reasons caused by sickness or accident it must be approved in advance by the Head of Division or, from this grade, by the next higher manager. In any event a completed leave form must be sent to the Directorate General Human Resources. Annual leave may be taken all at once or in several periods as desired by the member of staff and subject to business requirements. At least 20 days shall be taken before the end of the calendar year. This minimum of 20 days shall include at least one period of two consecutive weeks." 
    - Source: ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.2; 1 February 2026; p.93. (ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.2, p.93)
- The retrieved Context does not contain the full carry‑over ceiling, deadline to use carried days, explicit expiry wording for unused carried days, nor text on which authority may approve extensions beyond the carry‑over (other than the leave approval authority cited above). Insufficient retrieved source detail to provide the missing verbatim clauses. Please provide the relevant Staff Rules/Conditions pages if available.

- Available verbatim clauses in the retrieved Context:
  - "5.7.6 Conversely, leave taken in excess of entitlement will result in a deduction being made from the salary on the basis of one day’s salary for each day’s leave taken in excess of the leave entitlement." 
    - Source: ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.6; 1 February 2026; p.94. (ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.6, p.94)
  - "5.7.7 In calculating the amount for settling outstanding annual leave or annual leave taken in excess, a standard 21 working day month shall be used and one day’s annual leave shall be equivalent to one day’s payment. The period of leave outstanding will not count as service with the ECB and therefore no deductions for pension, ECB health insurance or accident insurance will be levied in respect of this period. Tax for the benefit of the European Communities will be deducted from these payments. The same method shall be applied to calculations in respect of annual leave overtaken." 
    - Source: ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.7; 1 February 2026; p.94. (ECB Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.7, p.94)

data.reasoning:
- Main rule: Annual leave entitlement is set out in Article 27 of the Conditions of Employment; procedural and settlement details are in the Staff Rules, Part 5, Article 5.7. Relevant text fragments in the retrieved Context and their locations:
  - Conditions of Employment, Article 27 — explicit entitlement figure (Page 13, Conditions of Employment, latest amendments entered into force 1 March 2026).
  - Staff Rules, Article 5.7.1–5.7.2 — pro rata calculation, approval authority (Head of Division / next higher manager), requirement to take at least 20 days within the calendar year and one two‑week period (Page 93, Staff Rules, 1 February 2026).
  - Staff Rules, Article 5.7.6–5.7.7 — consequences of leave in excess and settlement calculation using a standard 21 working‑day month; treatment of outstanding leave for service and deductions (Page 94, Staff Rules, 1 February 2026).
- Exceptions / variations requested (by grade, length of service, staff category; explicit “10.5 days carry‑over” wording; full carry‑over/expiry rules; exceptions for long‑term sickness, parental leave; full settlement limits) are not fully present in the retrieved Context. Only the passages above are available verbatim. Where the requested clause is absent from the provided excerpts, the response will state that the clause is not present in the supplied Context.
- Authorities: Head of Division or, from that grade, the next higher manager must approve leave (Staff Rules, Art. 5.7.2, p.93). Directorate General Human Resources is the recipient of completed leave forms (Art. 5.7.2, p.93). No other authority for carry‑over extensions appears in the retrieved excerpts.
- Timelines/figures in the retrieved Context: 30.5 working days per annum (Article 27, Conditions of Employment, p.13); pro rata calculation using 21 working days per month (Staff Rules, Art.5.7.1, p.93); at least 20 days to be taken before year‑end and one period of two consecutive weeks (Staff Rules, Art.5.7.2, p.93); settlement uses a 21 working‑day month and one day’s annual leave = one day’s payment (Staff Rules, Art.5.7.7, p.94).
- Reconciliation: Where both instruments appear, the Conditions of Employment provide the entitlement figure (most specific rule on entitlement) and the Staff Rules provide procedural application and calculation details (more specific for implementation). The most recent dates available in the retrieved Context are: Staff Rules version dated 1 February 2026; Conditions of Employment latest amendments entered into force 1 March 2026. No other amendment or temporary arrangement information is present in the retrieved Context.
